stories.title: Massachusetts House Okays Bill Allowing Residential on Church-Owned Land
stories.excerpt: The Massachusetts House of Representatives has voted to approve a bill that would allow churches, synagogues and other religious institutions to build housing on their property. Known as Yes in God’s Back Yard (YIGBY), the measure passed as part of a $561-million economic development and competitiveness bond bill. It needs to be approved by the Senate and signed by Gov. Maura Healey before becoming law. The YIGBY bill would allow religious entities to override local zoning and build housing on their properties without needing a special permit, reported the Boston Business Journal. It would add to a state program to build housing on surplus state-owned land and initiatives such as Boston’s office-to-apartment conversion incentive program. The Citizens’ Housing & Planning Association (CHAPA), which endorsed the YIGBY measure, applauded its passage in the House. CHAPA communications director Jenna Connolly called it “one of many innovative, creative solutions we need to provide more affordable housing across Massachusetts.” The post Massachusetts House Okays Bill Allowing Residential on Church-Owned Land appeared first on Connect CRE.
bullets: Massachusetts House approves YIGBY bill allowing churches to override zoning, build housing
Bill passes as part of $561M economic development bond; requires Senate approval, governor signature
Adds to state surplus-land program, Boston office-to-conversion initiatives to expand housing supply
stories.summary: The Massachusetts House voted to approve the Yes in God's Back Yard (YIGBY) bill, which permits religious institutions to construct housing on their properties without local zoning approval or special permits. The measure advanced as part of a $561 million economic development bond and complements existing state housing programs. It requires Senate approval and the governor's signature to become law.
stories.impact: If enacted, YIGBY meaningfully expands development-ready land supply in Massachusetts, particularly in high-zoning-constrained metros like Boston. Religious institutions control significant prime acreage; eliminating zoning barriers and special-permit delays reduces entitlement risk and timeline, accelerating supply pipeline in a state with chronic housing undersupply. Watch for site acquisition and pre-dev activity post-enactment.

stories.title: Airbnb Acquires Church Missions House in Gramercy for Reported $82M
stories.excerpt: Airbnb has finalized a deal to purchase 281 Park Ave South, the historic Church Missions House in Gramercy, for a reported $81.5 million. RFR was the seller. The six-story building is the first property that Airbnb has ever purchased in New York, a city that in 2021 enacted a law to further restrict short-term rentals. The Wall Street Journal reported that Airbnb, which already leases space in Lower Manhattan, acquired the 42,000-square-foot property as a roomier gathering place for its employees. The company employs more than 600 workers in the New York area. “New York City has been part of our story since the earliest days of Airbnb,” said CEO Brian Chesky. “This building reflects our long-term commitment to the city and will be home to one of our largest employee hubs outside of San Francisco. We’re excited to keep investing in the city and the people who make it extraordinary.” The post Airbnb Acquires Church Missions House in Gramercy for Reported $82M appeared first on Connect CRE.
bullets: Airbnb acquires 281 Park Ave South (Church Missions House) in Gramercy for $81.5M
Historic six-story, 42K sf building is Airbnb's first NYC real estate purchase; houses 600+ employees
Signals corporate commitment despite 2021 NYC short-term rental restrictions limiting Airbnb's core business
stories.summary: Airbnb purchased the Church Missions House at 281 Park Avenue South in Gramercy for $81.5 million, marking its first New York City property acquisition. The 42,000-square-foot, six-story historic building will serve as an expanded employee hub for over 600 workers in the region. The transaction underscores Airbnb's long-term commitment to New York despite the city's 2021 restrictions on short-term rentals.
stories.impact: Limited direct relevance to multifamily development. However, corporate real estate consolidation by tech/platform giants in Manhattan (Airbnb, American Express at WTC) increases office demand and competition for prime space, potentially raising acquisition costs for repositioning to residential or mixed-use in tight submarkets like Gramercy and Lower Manhattan.

stories.title: Target to Debut 128K-SF Firestone Store
stories.excerpt: Target has regained its luster. After a few years of declining sales, the company is on the rebound and is opening 300 new US stores by 2035. The Staenberg Group has announced that Target will open a new 128,000-square-foot store that will anchor Firestone City Centre, a 140-acre mixed-use development located in Firestone, roughly 30 miles north of Denver. The new store is scheduled to debut this month. Firestone City Centre features more than 600,000 square feet of retail and restaurant space that is home to a mix of national retailers and restaurants including The Home Depot and American Furniture Warehouse. Eleven more Targets are set to open across the country on July 26. Every one of them will exceed Target’s average store size of 125,000 square feet, and six will top 140,000 square feet. So far this year, Target has opened 13 stores. It plans to have 30 launched by the end of 2026. The post Target to Debut 128K-SF Firestone Store appeared first on Connect CRE.
bullets: Target opening 128K sf store at Firestone City Centre, 30 miles north of Denver; debuts July 2026
Retailer planning 300 new US stores by 2035; 30 stores by end of 2026, all exceeding 125K sf average
Firestone project anchors 140-acre mixed-use dev with 600K sf retail; signals continued expansion in tertiary markets
stories.summary: Target is opening a 128,000-square-foot anchor store at Firestone City Centre, a 140-acre mixed-use development roughly 30 miles north of Denver. The chain is executing an aggressive growth strategy, planning 300 new stores nationwide by 2035, with 30 open by end of 2026. All new stores exceed the company's historical 125,000-square-foot average.
stories.impact: Target's retail expansion into secondary/tertiary markets signals recovery in consumer spending and off-mall mixed-use viability in suburbs north of Denver and similar metros. This typically precedes multifamily density; strong anchor retail attracts foot traffic, supports mixed-use tenancy, and increases land value, creating windows for apartment development in adjacent parcels or future phases. Monitor Firestone and similar Staenberg projects for companion residential phases.

stories.title: Douglaston Realty Offloads 7 Cornelia Street in West Village for $25M
stories.excerpt: A business partner involved with the owner of the SoJo Spa Club in New Jersey signed mortgage documents tied to the sale of a West Village building, according to property records. While it’s unclear if Eun Rae Jo is involved in the $25 million purchase of 7 Cornelia Street, the involvement of Hyun Jun An, an investor and partner working alongside Jo, on the financing side raises the question of whether the famed spa owner has acquired yet another Manhattan property. The buyer in the deed transfer filed to property records on Thursday afternoon was masked behind a shell corporation lacking any indication of the beneficial owner. An was the authorized signatory on the $17.6 million mortgage documents from Woori America bank, according to property records. Douglaston Realty Management was the seller, alongside other entities that had a stake in the 2013 acquisition of the property for $24.6 million. Douglaston did not immediately respond to a request for comment. Neither Jo nor An could be reached for comment. Both have been working together on deals recently, including the acquisition of 893 Broadway in the Flatiron District for $31 million in early June and 16 East 18th Street from Revere Capital for $16.3 million the week prior. 7 Cornelia Street sits just south of West Fourth Street, about a block and a half west of Washington Square Park. The five-story prewar building has 47 apartments and two retail slots on the ground floor, according to StreetEasy. Worker-owned cocktail bar Donna occupies one of the two ground-floor spaces. Mark Hallum can be reached at mhallum@commercialobserver.com.
bullets: 7 Cornelia Street, West Village sold for $25M; buyer masked behind shell corporation
Hyun Jun An (SoJo Spa Club partner) signed $17.6M mortgage with Woori America Bank
Douglaston Realty sold property acquired in 2013 for $24.6M; minimal capital appreciation over 12 years
stories.summary: 7 Cornelia Street in Manhattan's West Village sold for $25 million with a $17.6 million mortgage from Woori America Bank. The buyer is a shell corporation, though financing documents show involvement of Hyun Jun An, an investor linked to SoJo Spa Club. Seller Douglaston Realty Management originally acquired the property in 2013 for $24.6 million.
stories.impact: Minimal relevance to multifamily development. The muted appreciation (0.2% CAGR over 12 years) amid strong Manhattan rental and condo markets suggests underlying property constraints (size, zoning, condition, or title issues). Use as market signal of soft pricing in niche West Village parcels if multifamily repositioning is contemplated.

stories.title: Caruso Sets Mid-August Reopening for Palisades Village
stories.excerpt: Caruso has set Saturday, August 15 for the grand reopening of Palisades Village, the Pacific Palisades shopping center that closed after the January 2025 wildfires swept through the community. The reopening follows more than $100 million in renovations to the 125,000-square-foot center, which currently is 99% leased. “Following the heartbreaking fires of 2025, the Palisades community has worked tirelessly toward this moment of renewal,” said Rick Caruso, founder and executive chairman of Caruso. “We promised to come back better than before the fires, and we did. This is a moment to turn the page together, and it will be an unforgettable one. It’s a comeback story for the history books and a love letter to L.A.” Returning tenants include Loomey’s Toys, K Bakery, Elysewalker, A.L.C., Erewhon, Zimmermann, Brunello Cucinelli, Veronica Beard, Angelini Ristorante & Bar, Alfred Coffee and Blue Ribbon Sushi. The reopening will also showcase new and first-in-market retailers, including the first West Coast location of LESET. The post Caruso Sets Mid-August Reopening for Palisades Village appeared first on Connect CRE.
bullets: Caruso's Palisades Village reopens August 15 post-January 2025 wildfires after $100M renovations
125K sf center 99% leased; includes returning luxury tenants (Brunello Cucinelli, Veronica Beard, Erewhon) plus new retailers
Signals rapid capital deployment and tenant confidence in LA Westside coastal recovery post-disaster
stories.summary: Caruso announced the August 15 reopening of Palisades Village after the January 2025 wildfires, following over $100 million in renovations. The 125,000-square-foot shopping center is 99% leased with returning anchors like Erewhon, luxury brands, and new tenants including the first West Coast LESET location.
stories.impact: Palisades Village reopening signals rapid capital redeployment in high-value LA Westside markets post-disaster and strong retail/mixed-use recovery demand. This creates parallel demand signals for adjacent multifamily: displaced residents rebuilding, ongoing gentrification, and mixed-use density incentives. Monitor Caruso's other LA-area properties and Westside parcels for companion or planned residential phases.

stories.title: Siemens Financial, First Horizon Lend $65M on Palm Beach Gardens Medical Complex
stories.excerpt: Siemens Financial and First Horizon have supplied $64.8 million of construction financing to develop a hospital and medical outpatient complex in Florida’s Palm Beach County, Commercial Observer has learned. Sina Companies secured the loan for the planned Health Park at Avenir project in Palm Beach Gardens, Fla., that is fully leased to Jupiter Medical Center. The Real Deal reported news of the loan on Thursday but did not identify the lender. Aztec Group negotiated the debt with a team led by Ezra Katz and Sean Harrington. Located at 12001 Northlake Boulevard, Health Park at Avenir will comprise a two-story neighborhood hospital and three-story medical outpatient building spanning 101,517 square feet. The project, which will be built within the 4,752-acre Avenir in Palm Beach Gardens master-planned community, includes an approved future phase for the development of 24,000 square feet of additional medical outpatient space, according to Sina. “We are excited to welcome Jupiter Medical Center to Avenir and further expand access to high-quality health care in Palm Beach Gardens,” Malcolm Sina, executive chairman of Sina Companies, said in a statement. Siemens Financial and First Horizon did not immediately return requests for comment. Andrew Coen can be reached at acoen@commercialobserver.com.
bullets: Siemens Financial, First Horizon lend $64.8M construction debt for Health Park at Avenir in Palm Beach Gardens
101.5K sf two-story hospital + three-story medical outpatient complex fully leased to Jupiter Medical Center
Project within 4,752-acre master-planned Avenir community; approved for future 24K sf additional medical space
stories.summary: Siemens Financial and First Horizon provided $64.8 million in construction financing for Health Park at Avenir, a 101,517-square-foot mixed medical facility in Palm Beach Gardens, Florida. The development includes a two-story neighborhood hospital and three-story outpatient building, fully leased to Jupiter Medical Center, within the larger Avenir master-planned community.
stories.impact: Signals strong institutional capital flow to specialized medical real estate in south Florida boom markets. Avenir's size (4,752 acres) and anchoring by anchor healthcare tenant creates gravitational pull for adjacent multifamily phases—hospitality and workforce housing near medical campuses commands premium rents. Monitor Avenir buildout plan for residential phases and timeline.

stories.title: Dimension Moving Ahead on 3-Building N. Phoenix Industrial Park
stories.excerpt: Dimension Financial Realty and Investments hopes to add its footprint to the burgeoning North Phoenix. The Phoenix Business Journal reports the company is proposing a new three-building spec industrial park with a 62,268-square-foot building, a 152,899-square-foot building and a 237,735-square-foot building. The more than 40-acre project site is located at the southwest corner of I-17 and Thunderbird Road – at 13610 N. Black Canyon Highway. It’s currently home to several vacant office buildings totaling 750,000 square-feet. In the 1960s the property was a tech campus for General Electric, and then later for Honeywell, according to a document for a PUD amendment at the site approved by the city of Phoenix last year. Dimension acquired the larger 160-acre master plan property – Metro North Corporate Park – back in 1994. The post Dimension Moving Ahead on 3-Building N. Phoenix Industrial Park appeared first on Connect CRE.
bullets: Dimension Financial proposes 1.2M sf industrial park (three spec buildings) at former GE/Honeywell tech campus, North Phoenix
70-acre site at I-17 and Thunderbird Road; formerly home to 750K sf vacant office space; Dimension owns larger 160-acre master plan
Project approved with conditions (sound wall); replaced earlier Amazon-linked 'Project Zeus' proposal (3M sf, five-story)
stories.summary: Dimension Financial Realty is advancing a three-building speculative industrial park totaling 1.2 million square feet on a 70-acre site at the southwest corner of I-17 and Thunderbird Road in North Phoenix. The property, formerly a GE and Honeywell tech campus with 750,000 square feet of vacant offices, sits within Dimension's larger 160-acre Metro North Corporate Park, acquired in 1994. The project was approved conditionally after replacing an earlier Amazon-linked proposal.
stories.impact: North Phoenix industrial growth signals continued infill densification and land repurposing in a historically office/tech market. Vacancy conversion to logistics/light industrial reduces speculative office risk but indicates office-to-industrial secular shift. Watch for companion multifamily demand: industrial growth attracts warehouse workers and logistics employment; monitor labor demographics and wage growth to assess rental demand in adjacent multifamily projects or future phases within Metro North.

stories.title: Twain Capital Supplies $65M C-PACE Loan for Colorado Hotel Project
stories.excerpt: Developer Nathaniel Kelly Foote has secured $65 million of Commercial Property Assessed Clean Energy (C-PACE) debt to complete a mixed-use project with a Marriott-branded hotel in the heart of Colorado’s Rocky Mountains, Commercial Observer has learned. Twain Capital Partners provided the C-PACE loan for the 144,597-square-foot Foote’s Rest development in Frisco, Colo., that will be anchored by an 80-key Marriott Tribute Hotel. The large-scale project will feature an underground 13,500-square-foot entertainment venue with bowling lanes and golf swing suites. It is slated for completion in October 2027. Andy Meyer, managing partner of Twain, said the deal was attractive from an underwriting perspective given Colorado’s strong C-PACE program that empowers property owners to fund 100 percent of sustainability upgrades coupled with solid hospitality demand in the Frisco market. He said Frsisco, which is 26 miles east of Vail and 15 minutes north of Breckenridge Ski Resort, has a lack of hotel supply. “Frisco continues to see more year-round travel,” Meyer told CO. “It was a challenging underwrite because there are no direct comps in the Frisco market, but a hotel of this size and scale with the amenities that are proposed we believe will achieve very strong occupancy.” Located at 500 Main Street, Foote’s Rest will also include a 4,000 square-foot ballroom, a 1,500 square feet of event space, a conference center, restaurants and a 10,000 square-foot rooftop bar. Colorado-based Greenwood Hospitality will serve as operator for the property. Foote did not immediately return a request for comment. The Foote’s Rest deal adds to Twain’s strong lending volume since the St. Louis-based lender launched a C-PACE business in late 2025 to complement its bridge lending platform. Meyer said the firm has executed $265 million of C-PACE loans over the past six months and has a pipeline $140 million under contract million that it expects to close by the end of 2026. Meyer, who joined Twa
bullets: Twain Capital supplies $65M C-PACE financing for Foote's Rest mixed-use project in Frisco, Colorado
80-key Marriott Tribute Hotel + 144.6K sf complex with underground 13.5K sf entertainment venue; completion October 2027
Project leverages Colorado's strong C-PACE program; Frisco (26 miles east of Vail, 15 min north of Breckenridge) has hotel supply shortage
stories.summary: Developer Nathaniel Kelly Foote secured $65 million in C-PACE financing from Twain Capital Partners for Foote's Rest, a 144,597-square-foot mixed-use project in Frisco, Colorado. The development will anchor an 80-key Marriott Tribute Hotel and includes an underground entertainment venue with bowling and golf suites, slated for completion October 2027. Twain cited Colorado's robust C-PACE program and strong hospitality demand in the underserved Frisco market.
stories.impact: Colorado C-PACE availability and Frisco's documented hotel supply shortage signal emerging destination hospitality demand in Rocky Mountain markets. This typically precedes workforce/seasonal multifamily demand. Frisco's proximity to Vail/Breckenridge and limited lodging create ancillary residential opportunity for ski-season labor and year-round service workers. Monitor hotel pipeline and occupancy post-Foote's Rest opening for spillover multifamily uptake.

stories.title: Event Company Glasshouses Inks 70K SF at 175 Greenwich Street
stories.excerpt: Event Company Glasshouses is establishing its flagship Lower Manhattan venue, Glasshouse Downtown, at the base of 3 World Trade Center. The company announced Thursday the execution of a long-term, 70,000-square-foot location at 175 Greenwich Street. The address is within the Westfield World Trade Center complex, owned by Paris-based developer and operator Unibail-Rodamco-Westfield (URW). “This is a defining moment for Westfield World Trade Center and the broader WTC campus,” Geoff Mason, executive vice president of U.S. development and operations at URW, said in a statement. “With Glasshouse bringing its world-class event space to Lower Manhattan, and the recent announcement that American Express will build its new global headquarters on campus, Westfield World Trade Center is evolving into an unmatched destination.” CBRE’s Anthony Dattoma, Chris Mansfield and Zachary Weil represented URW in the negotiations, according to the landlord. It is unclear whether Glasshouses worked with an outside brokerage. A spokesperson for CBRE did not immediately respond to a request for comment. Asking rent for the venue was not disclosed, but the build-out will require significant capital. The space is planned across two full floors of Tower 3 and is designed to accommodate nearly 1,500 guests. The venue will host corporate gatherings, conferences, nonprofit galas, productions and other large-scale social events. “Executing this long-term lease at the World Trade Center represents a defining milestone for our company,” Alex Holiday, a co-founder and partner at Glasshouses, said in a statement. Design and construction is slated for completion in 2028. Glasshouses previously launched venues Glasshouse Chelsea at 545 West 25th Street, the Glasshouse at 660 12th Avenue in Hell’s Kitchen, and GH on the Park at 54 West 40th Street in Midtown. Clarification: This article was updated to reflect the involvement of Zachary Weil in the transaction. Emily Davis can be reached
bullets: Glasshouses event company inks 70K sf at 175 Greenwich Street (base of 3 World Trade Center), Lower Manhattan
Long-term lease at Westfield World Trade Center complex; complements American Express new global HQ announcement on WTC campus
URW re-positioning WTC campus as mixed-use destination with event, corporate, and retail anchors
stories.summary: Event company Glasshouses secured a long-term, 70,000-square-foot lease for its flagship Glasshouse Downtown venue at 175 Greenwich Street, within the Westfield World Trade Center complex. The announcement follows American Express's recent declaration of a new global headquarters on the WTC campus, signaling URW's strategic repositioning of the complex as a mixed-use destination combining office, retail, and hospitality.
stories.impact: American Express HQ relocation to WTC (with Glasshouses event anchor) signals major corporate consolidation and mixed-use intensification in Lower Manhattan. This drives employment density, foot traffic, and evening/weekend activation—all support factors for adjacent multifamily demand. Monitor HQ phasing, lease commencement, and employee count to time multifamily development/acquisitions in Battery Park, Tribeca, and Financial District submarkets.

stories.title: Redstone Bank, Goldman Sachs, New York State Provide $217M for Alafia Project
stories.excerpt: A new affordable housing complex in one of Brooklyn’s poorest neighborhoods just took another step closer to completion thanks to a combination of private dollars and public financial support. Apex Building Group and L+M Development Partners have secured $217 million in construction financing for the third phase of the Alafia Project, a two-building residential development that will deliver 273 units of affordable housing and new public green space to East New York, Brooklyn, Commercial Observer can first report. Redstone Bank, working with Goldman Sachs Urban Investment Group, and the New York State Energy Research and Development Authority provided an undisclosed amount of construction debt. New York State Homes and Community Renewal — the state’s umbrella affordable housing agency and housing finance entity — is expected to provide $97 million in equity through the federal Low-Income Housing Tax Credit program, while the state’s Low-Income Housing Tax Credit is expected to deliver $14 million in equity and an additional $57 million in tax-exempt bonds for the project’s remaining equity. “The $217 million investment includes modern and sustainable apartments, as well as recreational and green space that promote togetherness,” said RuthAnne Visnauskas, commissioner of New York State Homes and Community Renewal. The greater Alafia redevelopment project will eventually transform 27 acres of the old Brooklyn Developmental Center, a former state institution for individuals with intellectual disabilities, into a mix of new affordable housing, community centers and mixed-use retail developments for residents of East New York. The neighborhood’s poverty rate hit nearly 22 percent in 2024, compared to 18 percent citywide, according to New York University. “As this next phase moves forward, hundreds of additional affordable homes, thoughtfully designed public spaces and sustainable infrastructure will help strengthen East New York,” said Hope Knight, commissioner, president
bullets: Redstone Bank, Goldman Sachs UIG, NY State fund $217M construction debt for Alafia Project Phase 3, East New York, Brooklyn
273 units affordable housing (two buildings) + public green space; NYS Low-Income Tax Credit providing $97M equity, $14M state LIHTC, $57M tax-exempt bonds
Part of broader SomeraRoad East New York revitalization; earlier phases include 278 apts (2024) and 84K sf retail/office
stories.summary: Apex Building Group and L+M Development Partners secured $217 million in construction financing for Phase 3 of the Alafia Project in East New York, Brooklyn, delivering 273 units of affordable housing across two buildings plus public green space. Redstone Bank, Goldman Sachs Urban Investment Group, and New York State Energy Research and Development Authority provided construction debt; NYS contributed $97 million in Low-Income Housing Tax Credit equity, $14 million in state LIHTC, and $57 million in tax-exempt bonds.
stories.impact: Public-private capital stack (Goldman Sachs + Redstone + NYS LIHTC/bonds) for 273-unit affordable phase signals scaled multifamily production capability in East New York and renewed investor appetite for LIHTC-backed development post-housing shortage policy focus. SomeraRoad's broader East New York buildout (278 apts 2024, Phase 3 now funding) demonstrates successful catalytic redevelopment. Monitor pipeline and Phase 4/5 for acquisition/financing windows in emerging East Brooklyn submarkets.

stories.title: Vernon Hills clears 1.2M sf industrial redevelopment on former American Hotel Register site
stories.excerpt: Redevelopment of an ex-American Hotel Register property northwest of Chicago was approved,  greenlighting an additional 1 million-square-foot industrial park in the region.  The Vernon Hills site spans 70 acres and is set for 1.2 million square feet spread across three speculative buildings, one existing warehouse and a site for hand2mind, a classroom supplies and teacher curriculum company. An entity listed as JCA Hayes LLC has been trying to get this project in motion since late 2024, according to the Chicago Daily Herald. The planned site sits at 100-230 South Milwaukee Avenue.  At one point, it appeared that the land was […]This article originally appeared on The Real Deal. Click here to read the full story.
bullets: Vernon Hills approves 1.2M sf industrial redevelopment on 70-acre former American Hotel Register site (100-230 South Milwaukee Avenue)
JCA Hayes LLC developer proposes three spec warehouses + existing warehouse + hand2mind classroom supplies HQ; conditioned on sound wall
Site previously eyed for Amazon 'Project Zeus' (3M sf, five-story office/warehouse); Amazon proposal withdrawn, project recast as smaller spec industrial
stories.summary: Vernon Hills approved a 1.2 million-square-foot industrial redevelopment on a 70-acre former American Hotel Register site northwest of Chicago. JCA Hayes LLC is developing three speculative warehouses, retaining one existing building, and including a hand2mind classroom supplies company headquarters. The project replaced an earlier, much larger Amazon-linked 'Project Zeus' proposal (3 million square feet, five-story), which was withdrawn after local opposition.
stories.impact: Chicago-area industrial infill approval signals continued warehouse/logistics demand in secondary rings. However, Amazon withdrawal and downsize from 3M sf to 1.2M sf suggests cooling e-commerce real estate appetite or higher capital discipline post-2023. Monitor absorption and leasing pace for Chicagoland multifamily demand signals; warehouse-sector job growth (lower-wage, distributed) typically supports workforce multifamily in suburban corridors. Hand2mind's HQ consolidation signals corporate footprint optimization, not net job creation.

stories.title: Caterpillar digs into new $34M HQ expansion in Las Colinas
stories.excerpt: Caterpillar, a worldwide construction and engineering manufacturer that calls suburban Dallas home, is planning an expansion of its headquarters around three miles down the road from its current one at the Towers at Williams Square. The company is expected to spend $36 million to renovate portions of an office property at 901 West Walnut Hill Lane in Irving. Initial phases of the project are slated to cover 107,000 square feet and are expected to be completed around August 2027, according to the Dallas Morning News. The total square footage of the Las Colinas offices is pegged at over 400,000 square […]This article originally appeared on The Real Deal. Click here to read the full story.
bullets: Caterpillar invests $34M (some sources $36M) to expand Las Colinas HQ at former Zales campus, Irving, Texas
107K sf Phase 1 renovation (400K+ sf total campus); completion August 2027; company employs growing workforce at new facility
Caterpillar relocated Dallas from Deerfield, Illinois in 2022; expanding indicates sustained demand for additional office, dealer, customer space
stories.summary: Caterpillar is investing approximately $34–36 million to renovate and expand its headquarters at a former Zales/Vari office campus at 901 West Walnut Hill Lane in Irving, Texas (Las Colinas), just three miles from its current Towers at Williams Square location. The initial 107,000-square-foot phase is slated for completion August 2027, with the total campus spanning over 400,000 square feet. The expansion accommodates workforce growth and provides space for dealer and customer engagement.
stories.impact: Caterpillar's Dallas HQ expansion (post-2022 relocation from Deerfield) signals sustained North Texas corporate migration and office clustering in Las Colinas/Irving submarket. Growing CAT workforce and demand for dealer/customer space indicate indirect job/population inflow. Monitor companion service, logistics, and supplier clustering; this typically precedes strong rental demand for workforce multifamily in Irving, Las Colinas, and surrounding suburbs. Watch Phase 2+ expansion timelines.

stories.title: Pendry Nashville enlists Douglas Elliman, broker to woo New York condo buyers
stories.excerpt: A development advisory firm is wagering that a formula crafted in New York combining an intimate live music concert series with philanthropy can draw interest to a high-end Nashville condo project.  Redeavor, the two-year-old firm serving as the exclusive sales and marketing team for the Pendry Residences Nashville, has tapped Douglas Elliman and one of its longtime brokers, Greg Williamson, as New York City marketing partners.  Developed by SomeraRoad, the 30-story, $364 million tower is slated to include 146 condo units on the upper floors, as well as a 180-key hotel. Redeavor’s Maranda Blanton said they anticipate construction will be […]This article originally appeared on The Real Deal. Click here to read the full story.
bullets: Pendry Residences Nashville enlists Douglas Elliman, Greg Williamson (Soho Sessions founder) for NYC condo marketing
30-story, $364M mixed-use tower: 146 condos (from $1.3M–$7M penthouse), 180-key hotel; completion end-2027, hotel opens early-2028
Closed $26M pre-sales Q1 2026; project anchors SomeraRoad's Paseo South Gulch redevelopment (84K sf retail/office + 278-apt 2024 opening)
stories.summary: Redeavor, the sales and marketing partner for Pendry Residences Nashville, hired Douglas Elliman and broker Greg Williamson to target New York City condo buyers. The 30-story, $364 million SomeraRoad development includes 146 residential condos (priced from $1.3 million to $7 million) and a 180-key hotel, with completion targeted for end-2027 and hotel opening early-2028. The project closed $26 million in pre-sales in Q1 2026 and anchors the broader Paseo South Gulch mixed-use revitalization.
stories.impact: Pendry Nashville's NYC marketing push and $26M Q1 pre-sales signal strong East Coast capital flows into Nashville Gulch redevelopment. SomeraRoad's portfolio approach (278-apt complex 2024, Pendry 2028, retail/office) demonstrates catalytic mixed-use absorption and is creating precedent for follow-on multifamily phases. Monitor broader Gulch supply pipeline and pricing power to inform valuation/positioning for competing developments in emerging Nashville submarkets.

stories.title: Miami Freedom Park redevelopment, West Palm office tower among $370M-plus construction permits for week ending July 8, 2026
stories.excerpt: 🏆 New Building: The largest permit issued was a $193 million new construction permit for 1822 Northwest 37th Avenue in Miami, tied to the redevelopment of Miami Freedom Park near the Miami International Airport, which will include more than 1 million square feet of retail, dining, entertainment and office space, 750 hotel rooms, a 58-acre public park, a new City of Miami administration building and Inter Miami CF’s Nu Stadium — which is already complete.Job Filing Number: BD25026033 📊 New Building: The second-largest permit was a $180 million commercial permit for 850 South Quadrille Boulevard in West Palm Beach, where […]This article originally appeared on The Real Deal. Click here to read the full story.
bullets: Miami Freedom Park redevelopment: $193M permit for 1.8M sf mixed-use (retail, dining, entertainment, office, hotel, stadium, park)
West Palm Point office tower: $180M permit; Cohen Bros. project stalled for years, foreclosure lawsuit 2025; now moving forward
South Florida permits week of 7/8/26 exceed $370M; signals continued mixed-use and office investment despite past stalls
stories.summary: Miami issued a $193 million permit for Miami Freedom Park redevelopment near Miami International Airport, encompassing over 1 million square feet of retail, dining, entertainment, office, and 750 hotel rooms, plus a 58-acre public park and Inter Miami CF's completed Nu Stadium. West Palm Beach simultaneously issued a $180 million permit for West Palm Point, a stalled office tower that faced foreclosure in 2025 but is now advancing. Combined with other permits, South Florida exceeded $370 million in construction permits for the week ending July 8, 2026.
stories.impact: Miami Freedom Park's scale (1M+ sf mixed-use, 750 hotel rooms, public park) near MIA signals major employment/hospitality anchor and airport-adjacent revitalization. This typically attracts workforce multifamily in adjacent airport-corridor markets. West Palm Point's recovery from foreclosure/stall indicates capital repricing and renewed office investment, though office fundamentals remain weak; monitor for eventual office-to-residential conversion pipelines. Both markets show capital returning to previously distressed South Florida projects.

stories.title: Naterra eyeing a $34M combination office and warehouse in Coppell
stories.excerpt: Naterra International, a cosmetics and beauty company, is looking to expand their operations in Coppell with a new $34 million combination headquarters and warehouse facility.  The facility is expected to span 442,010 square feet, with no additional information on how the space will be split between the office and the warehouse, according to the Dallas Business Journal.  Dallas-based Pross Design Group will be heading up the construction. The future headquarters of the company will sit at 1525 Point West Boulevard in the suburb just northwest of Dallas, and the facility should be completed around December 2027. The price of the […]This article originally appeared on The Real Deal. Click here to read the full story.
bullets: Naterra International (cosmetics/beauty) invests $34M in 442K sf headquarters + warehouse combo in Coppell, Texas
Completion December 2027; $77/sf cost; family-owned, 30-year Walmart supplier with brands (Baby Magic, Tree Hut, Splash)
Coppell hosts Mary Kay, Sally Beauty, Jack Black HQ; Dallas-Fort Worth beauty/cosmetics cluster trend continues
stories.summary: Naterra International, a family-owned global cosmetics manufacturer, is building a $34 million, 442,010-square-foot headquarters and warehouse facility in Coppell, Texas, slated for completion December 2027. The company, a longtime Walmart supplier with brands including Baby Magic and Tree Hut, is consolidating operations from its current Coppell location. The project costs approximately $77 per square foot.
stories.impact: Naterra's corporate consolidation in Coppell (vs. relocation out) signals DFW retains office/light-industrial clusters. Dallas-Fort Worth beauty/personal care sector clustering (Mary Kay, Sally Beauty, Jack Black, Cetaphil) creates dedicated employment ecosystems. Monitor Coppell workforce migration and wage trends; corporate HQ consolidation typically supports wage growth, attracting multifamily. Watch for competing/complementary industrial/office projects in northwest DFW suburbs for supply/demand windows.

stories.title: San Antonio closes on three key Project Marvel parcels for $30M
stories.excerpt: San Antonio formally acquired federal properties downtown that are within the boundaries of a potential sports and entertainment district dubbed Project Marvel.  Officials for the General Services Administration and the city announced Monday that San Antonio paid $30 million for the federal office building at 727 East César Chávez Boulevard and two parking lots on Indianola Street. The purchase was funded by the NBA franchise the San Antonio Spurs, city manager Erik Walsh said in a statement. The San Antonio Business Journal reported that part of Project Marvel is set aside for the Spurs, who plan on building a new […]This article originally appeared on The Real Deal. Click here to read the full story.
bullets: San Antonio closes on three GSA parcels for $30M, funded by San Antonio Spurs, for Project Marvel sports/entertainment district
Acquisition includes federal office building (727 E. César Chávez Blvd) + two parking lots; Spurs plan new arena in district
Project Marvel total budget $1.2B–$1.5B; includes expanded convention center, 50 acres mixed-use, 1,000-room hotel; Frost Bank Center to become rodeo district
stories.summary: San Antonio officially acquired three federal properties—a federal office building at 727 East César Chávez Boulevard and two parking lots—for $30 million, funded by the NBA San Antonio Spurs. The purchase is part of Project Marvel, a $1.2–1.5 billion sports and entertainment district anchored by a new Spurs arena. The broader project includes expanded convention center, 50 acres of mixed-use development, and a 1,000-room hotel.
stories.impact: Spurs-funded arena district (Project Marvel $1.2–1.5B) is a major anchor catalyzing downtown San Antonio redevelopment. Sports/entertainment districts typically generate employment, foot traffic, and event-driven demand supporting premium multifamily; hotel, retail, and convention expansion create hospitality and workforce jobs. Monitor Project Marvel phasing, arena financing, and mixed-use parcel RFPs for adjacent/follow-on multifamily development windows and timing-to-rental-demand curves in downtown SA market.
